name: omnigent-vscode
description: Omnigent in VS Code — embed the Omnigent SPA with native editor commands.

omnigent.serverUrl.markdownDescription: Manual Omnigent server URL override (e.g. `https://omnigent.example.com` or `http://127.0.0.1:6767`). When set, this takes precedence over local auto-discovery. Leave empty to auto-discover a locally running Omnigent server.

omnigent.token.markdownDescription: Optional bearer token override. **Prefer the CLI token** stored in `~/.omnigent/auth_tokens.json` (auto-resolved) over setting a token here. When set, this takes precedence over the file token. Treat as a secret; it is never logged.

omnigent.defaultAgentId.markdownDescription: Default agent id (e.g. `ag_…`) used when creating a session. When set, the agent picker is skipped. Leave empty to be prompted to choose an agent from the server's `GET /v1/agents` list each time you open a session.

omnigent.defaultAgentName.markdownDescription: Informational name of your preferred agent. Display-only — session creation uses `#omnigent.defaultAgentId#`. Use this to remember which agent the id refers to.

enumDescriptions: Host the running Omnigent server inside an iframe (default). Requires a local server; no token is ever placed in the URL.
Mount the bundled Omnigent SPA (embed) directly. Experimental/quarantined — requires the embed assets to be present.
omnigent.renderMode.markdownDescription: How the Omnigent panel renders. `iframe` (default) loads the running local server; `embed` mounts the bundled SPA. The iframe path is used only for local servers; remote/unknown servers fall back to the embed path.

enumDescriptions: Reveal the Omnigent panel docked on the right (best-effort move to the secondary side bar; VS Code remembers your placement afterwards).
Open Omnigent in an editor tab beside the active editor (the guaranteed always-right option).
Reveal the Omnigent panel in its activity-bar container on the left (today's default placement).
omnigent.panelLocation.markdownDescription: Where **Omnigent: Open** reveals the panel. `right` (default) docks to the secondary side bar; `editor` opens a beside-editor tab (most reliable for a right-hand pane); `left` keeps the activity-bar view.
